SEARCH
Package compiler.ast

Interface ASTVisitor<T>

Type Parameters:
T - the return type produced by each visit method
All Known Implementing Classes:
AsciiTreePrinter, MutatorVisitor, PrettyPrinter
public interface ASTVisitor<T>
Interface for visiting AST nodes and performing operations on them. The Visitor design pattern allows us to implement new operations (printing, analysis, interpretation, code generation) without modifying the AST node classes. Each visit method corresponds to a specific AST node type and can perform type-specific logic. Implementations of this interface are called by ASTNode.accept(ASTVisitor) to perform some operation on the AST. Multiple visitors can coexist:
  • AsciiTreePrinter: prints the tree structure
  • PrettyPrinter: formats the source code
  • MutatorVisitor: applies transformations
  • Custom visitors: analysis, code generation, interpretation
See Also:
  • ASTNode.accept(ASTVisitor)
  • Program
  • Rule

  • Method Details

    • visit

      T visit(Program program)
      Visits a complete program node (top-level AST root).
      Parameters:
      program - the program containing all rules
      Returns:
      visitor-defined result for a program

    • visit

      T visit(Rule rule)
      Visits an individual rule node (condition + command pair). Provides rule-level granularity for policies or analyses that need to reason about rules as distinct units rather than just their constituent parts.
      Parameters:
      rule - the rule to visit
      Returns:
      visitor-defined result for a rule

    • visit

      T visit(ActionNode action)
      Visits an action command node.
      Parameters:

      Returns:
      visitor-defined result

      Parameters:

      Returns:
      visitor-defined result

      Parameters:

      Returns:
      visitor-defined result
